---
title: "Methodology"
subtitle: "How this site is built, where its data come from, and how often it refreshes"
date: last-modified
---

# Architecture

The site has three coupled layers:

1. **Static Quarto site** — all content pages (course catalogue,
   designation pathways, learning outcomes, skills scaffold, methodology,
   announcements). Authored in markdown / qmd; rendered to HTML and
   served as static files.
2. **Shiny degree planner** — the interactive component, embedded as
   an iframe in the static site. Lets a student drag courses into year
   slots, pick a designation goal, and see real-time gap analysis.
3. **`targets` data pipeline** — daily scrape of the UBCO Academic
   Calendar, normalisation to a tidy parquet, plus the curated
   designation-mapping YAMLs. Output artefacts feed both the Quarto
   site (via embedded chunks) and the Shiny planner.

# Data sources and refresh cadence

| Feed | Source URL | How | Cadence |
|---|---|---|---|
| Course descriptions | `okanagan.calendar.ubc.ca/course-descriptions/subject/<code>` | `rvest` scrape, per-subject | Daily, 12:00 UTC |
| Program structure | `okanagan.calendar.ubc.ca/.../freshwater-science` | `rvest` scrape | Daily, 12:00 UTC |
| Course schedule (offered next year?) | UBCO Workday Student / SSC | TBD — scrape vs. API | Daily, 12:00 UTC |
| BCIA PAg UBCO-approved list | `bcia.com/sites/.../UBCO Approved Courses_*.pdf` | Manual OCR + curated YAML | Triggered on PDF update (BCIA edits the file ~annually) |
| CAB RPBio Credentialing Standard | `cab-bc.org/wp-content/uploads/Credentialing_Standard_*.pdf` | Manual OCR + curated YAML | Triggered on standard update |
| Program learning outcomes | Faculty of Science / EESC departmental docs | Manual transcription | Annual |
| Course learning outcomes | Instructor interviews | Manual collection | Per-course, on rolling basis |

# Scrape policy

UBCO's calendar is publicly accessible. The scraper respects the
following constraints:

- Single sequential scrape (no parallel requests) with a polite
  `User-Agent: limnology.ca UBCO Freshwater Pathways v0.x`.
- Per-request delay of 1 second between subject pages.
- Local cache (file mtime check) so a re-run within the same day is a
  no-op.
- All scraping happens server-side in GitHub Actions, never in the
  user's browser.

If UBCO's registrar office can provide a data feed or API, the scraper
will be retired in favour of that.

# Designation-mapping authority

The **PAg** and **RPBio** mappings are not scraped. The source documents
(BCIA UBCO list, CAB Credentialing Standard) are PDF publications by
the respective regulatory bodies. The workflow:

1. Download the latest PDF when published.
2. OCR locally to a text file in `docs/reference/`.
3. Hand-curate the UBCO course-code -> designation-category map into
   `data/designation_mappings/pag.yml` and `.../rpbio.yml`.
4. Versioned, dated YAMLs so historical pathways are preserved.

This preserves traceability: every course-counts-for-X claim on the
site is backed by a citable, dated source.

# Pipeline reproducibility

The `targets` pipeline plus the GitHub Actions cron workflow give:

- Full provenance for every artefact (which scrape run, which source
  version, which curator).
- Deterministic re-renders.
- Public commit history of when designation mappings changed.

# Stack inventory

| Component | Library |
|---|---|
| Site | Quarto 1.6+ |
| Scraping | `rvest`, `httr2`, `xml2` |
| Tidying | `dplyr`, `tidyr`, `stringr`, `lubridate` |
| Storage | `arrow` (parquet) |
| Pipeline | `targets` |
| Planner | `shiny`, `bslib`, `DT`, `visNetwork` (prereq graph) |
| Validation | `assertr`, `testthat` |
| Deploy | GitHub Actions, shinyapps.io (planner), `limnology.ca` (static site) |
